items.title: PAN-OS GlobalProtect Auth Bypass (CVE-2026-0257): Active Exploitation
items.desc: Palo Alto PAN-OS GlobalProtect gateway flaw allows unauthenticated access; under active exploitation.
items.summary: CVE-2026-0257 in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS GlobalProtect gateway allows remote attackers to bypass authentication and access internal networks. The flaw affects multiple PAN-OS versions and is being actively exploited in the wild, targeting enterprise VPN infrastructure globally.
items.importance: PAN-OS GlobalProtect is a primary enterprise VPN for remote access. A bypass here means anyone on the internet can walk into your network perimeter.
items.noteworthy: Patch PAN-OS to 10.2.14-h3/11.1.6/12.1.3. Disable GlobalProtect if not strictly required. Audit VPN logs for anomalous auth patterns.

items.title: Dutch Botnet Takedown: 17M Infected Devices Neutralized
items.desc: Authorities dismantled a massive IoT botnet; highlights the scale of compromised consumer hardware.
items.summary: Dutch police and international partners have dismantled a botnet responsible for compromising 17 million devices globally. The botnet was primarily used for DDoS attacks and credential stuffing.
items.importance: 17M devices is a wake-up call. Your IoT VLAN isolation isn't paranoia—it's basic hygiene.
items.noteworthy: Check your Pi-hole and router for unusual outbound traffic. Rotate all credentials on devices that haven't been patched in 6+ months.

items.title: ChatGPhish: ChatGPT Web Summaries Weaponized for Phishing
items.desc: New attack uses LLM web summarization as a phishing vector; tricks users into visiting malicious links.
items.summary: Researchers found that ChatGPT's web content summarization feature can be manipulated to display fake URLs. Attackers embed malicious links in web pages that, when summarized, appear legitimate.
items.importance: LLMs are now a phishing delivery mechanism. As you integrate AI into workflows, the 'trust the AI output' assumption is a liability.
items.noteworthy: Never click URLs from LLM-summarized content without verifying. Use Pali's URL preview before trusting AI-generated links.

items.title: LLM Agents Used for Post-Exploitation After Marimo RCE
items.desc: Attackers chain Marimo CVE-2026-39987 RCE with autonomous LLM agents for post-exploitation.
items.summary: A new attack chain uses the Marimo CVE-2026-39987 RCE exploit to gain initial access, then deploys an LLM agent to autonomously search for valuable files, API keys, and credentials on the compromised system.
items.importance: AI is now being used OFFENSIVELY. Attackers are automating post-exploitation with AI agents. The asymmetry is growing.
items.noteworthy: Patch Marimo immediately. Monitor for anomalous LLM API calls from internal hosts. Use network egress filtering to prevent unauthorized AI API usage.

items.title: Kimsuky Deploys HTTPSpy, Expands with HelloDoor & VS Code Tunnels
items.desc: North Korean Kimsuky APT adds new tools; uses VS Code Tunnels as a covert C2 channel.
items.summary: Kimsuky has introduced HTTPSpy (a custom backdoor) and is leveraging VS Code's remote tunnels feature as a novel C2 channel that bypasses traditional network monitoring.
items.importance: VS Code Tunnels is a new C2 vector. It's inside your allowed software list. The 'living off the land' playbook is evolving.
items.noteworthy: Disable VS Code's remote tunnel feature in enterprise environments. Audit VS Code extensions. Block *.service.twilio.com and *.tunnel.dev inside the perimeter.

items.title: Malicious NuGet Package Steals Banking Credentials
items.desc: Sicoob malware targets .NET developers via poisoned NuGet packages; harvests banking credentials.
items.summary: A malicious NuGet package is stealing developer credentials and environment variables. It specifically targets .NET applications used in the Brazilian banking sector.
items.importance: NuGet is the next NPM. The same attack patterns are migrating to the .NET ecosystem.
items.noteworthy: Audit NuGet package sources. Pin NuGet versions. Monitor .env files for unauthorized access.

items.title: Market: BTC $73,786. Market Still Bleeding.
items.summary: Crypto markets remain in a risk-off rotation. No clear catalyst for reversal. ETF inflows flat.
items.importance: The bear market is testing patience. Hold cash. Watch $70K floor.
items.noteworthy: No new positions. Monitor $70K BTC support. SOL losing relative strength.

items.title: GREYVIBE: Russia-Linked APT Targets Ukraine with AI-Powered Attacks
items.desc: New Russian APT 'GREYVIBE' uses AI for spear-phishing and social engineering against Ukrainian targets.
items.summary: Russian state-sponsored actors are using AI to automate highly targeted spear-phishing campaigns against Ukrainian government and critical infrastructure.
items.importance: State actors are fully weaponizing AI. The 'Cyber War' playbook is being rewritten in real-time.
items.noteworthy: If you monitor regional threats, deploy AI-aware email filtering. Watch for AI-generated spear-phish that bypasses traditional detection.

items.title: npm Malware Harvests Claude AI User Data via GitHub Tokens
items.desc: Rogue npm package steals Claude AI project files and GitHub tokens from developer machines.
items.summary: A malicious npm package was found actively exfiltrating data from Claude AI user directories, specifically targeting project files, conversation histories, and stored GitHub Personal Access Tokens (PATs). The package used GitHub Actions workflows and GitHub Gist APIs as a covert C2 channel to beam data out of compromised machines. It was specifically designed to target developers using Anthropic's Claude CLI or desktop application, harvesting project-specific context that could be used for corporate espionage or account takeover.
items.importance: This is a direct attack on the 'AI Coding' ecosystem. By targeting Claude users, the attacker is targeting the most valuable class of developers—those working on sensitive proprietary projects. A single stolen PAT can cascade into a full software supply chain compromise.
items.noteworthy: Rotate all GitHub PATs immediately if you use the Claude CLI. Audit your Claude project directories for unauthorized network connections. Use Claude's built-in 'sandboxed execution' mode for untrusted projects. Implement network egress monitoring on developer machines to detect data exfiltration to GitHub Gist domains.

items.title: GlassWorm Malware Takedown: Google, GitHub & CISA Disrupt AI Model Theft
items.desc: Joint operation dismantles GlassWorm infrastructure; was targeting AI model weights and LLM training datasets.
items.summary: Google, GitHub, and CISA have jointly disrupted the infrastructure behind the GlassWorm supply chain attack campaign. GlassWorm was specifically designed to target AI model repositories, stealing training datasets, model weights, and fine-tuning data from developer machines. The takedown removes a major threat from the AI ecosystem, though investigators warn that the 'Agentic' era of AI has made these supply chain attacks significantly more dangerous and harder to detect.
items.importance: GlassWorm represents the evolution of supply chain attacks from 'code theft' to 'AI theft.' As we move to more agentic workflows where AI has access to file systems and APIs, a compromise in the training data or model weights can be propagated into the model's decision-making process.
items.noteworthy: Verify your model repositories and training data directories for any unauthorized modifications. Implement file integrity monitoring on HuggingFace cache directories. Use hardware-backed keys for model uploads. The GlassWorm takedown is a win, but expect copycat campaigns to emerge shortly.

items.title: Grandoreiro & BTMOB RAT: Cross-Platform Banking Trojans Surge
items.desc: New wave of Grandoreiro (Windows) and BTMOB (Android) RAT campaigns targeting MENA banking apps.
items.summary: ESET researchers have identified a resurgence of the Grandoreiro banking trojan (Windows) and a new Android variant called BTMOB. Grandoreiro uses sophisticated PDF injection overlays to steal from Brazilian and Mexican banks, while BTMOB hijacks AndroidAccessibilityServices to intercept OTPs and perform overlay attacks on regional banking apps. Both campaigns are operated by the same financially-motivated threat actor, indicating a cross-platform convergence strategy for maximum financial theft.
items.importance: As you manage digital assets and work with financial APIs in your projects, the 'Financial RAT' sector is your primary threat model for monetary loss. The Android variant specifically targets your Pixel/Mobile testing environment if you have banking apps installed.
items.noteworthy: On Android (Pixel 8a): Remove banking apps from the primary device or use a dedicated 'burner' device for financial transactions. On Windows: Do not disable SmartScreen and ensure your browser is configured to warn on PDF-based overlays. Monitor your financial accounts for unusual OTP requests.

items.title: LiteSpeed cPanel Root RCE (CVE-2026-48172): Patch Immediately
items.desc: Active exploitation of LiteSpeed Enterprise cPanel extension allows privilege escalation to root.
items.summary: The LiteSpeed Web Server Enterprise cPanel extension (LScp) contains a critical privilege escalation flaw (CVE-2026-48172). Authenticated cPanel users can abuse the extension's terminal plugin to execute arbitrary commands with root privileges on the host system. Active exploitation is confirmed in the wild, primarily targeting shared hosting environments where a single user can pivot to full control of the physical server.
items.importance: This is a 'Tenant-to-Host' escape—arguably the worst-case scenario for shared hosting. If your lab or projects use LiteSpeed/cPanel, assume the attacker is already inside the perimeter.
items.noteworthy: Patch LiteSpeed cPanel extension to version 1.10.2+ immediately. Audit /usr/local/CellController/ for unauthorized modifications. If patching is not possible, disable the LiteSpeed terminal plugin in WHM. Consider migrating to a non-cPanel stack (like Coolify or Yunohost) if you need a lightweight self-hosted alternative.

items.title: npm Enforces Mandatory 2FA for Publishers—Security Win
items.desc: npm registry mandates two-factor authentication for all package publishers; blocks malicious post-install scripts.
items.summary: npm has officially rolled out mandatory two-factor authentication (2FA) for all package publishers, along with new 'Package Install Controls' that allow maintainers to block post-install scripts entirely. The change comes after multiple supply chain attacks (including the recent Claude-targeting malware) exploited the trust developers place in the npm registry. The enforcement is expected to significantly reduce the success rate of typosquatting and token-theft-based package poisoning campaigns.
items.importance: This is a fundamental shift in the security posture of the world's largest package registry. By making 2FA mandatory for publishers, npm is closing the primary vector for token-theft attacks that plagued the ecosystem in 2024-2025.
items.noteworthy: If you maintain an npm package, enable 2FA immediately. Use the new 'Install Controls' to block scripts from packages you don't trust. Monitor your npm dashboard for any unauthorized package updates. This is a positive step, but don't let it create a false sense of security—audit your dependencies regardless.

items.title: YouTube Auto-Labels AI-Generated Content—Deepfake Defense Layer
items.desc: YouTube mandates AI-generated content labels for creators; aims to curb deepfake misinformation and synthetic media.
items.summary: YouTube has launched a mandatory disclosure system requiring creators to label AI-generated or synthetically altered content. The system focuses on 'realistic' content—videos depicting people saying things they never said or events that never happened. The label will be displayed as a prominent badge on the video and in search results. This is a direct response to the wave of AI-generated political deepfakes and synthetic news content that proliferated during recent elections.
items.importance: As the 'NetOps' project grows and you produce content or training materials, understanding the new 'AI content provenance' requirements is essential. Synthetic media is becoming a regulatory target globally.
items.noteworthy: When producing training or demo content for your projects, clearly label any AI-generated elements to comply with upcoming regulations. Monitor the YouTube policy for 'realistic content' exemptions—subtle AI editing may also trigger the requirement.

items.title: Market Update: BTC Holds $73K Amid ETF Outflow Panic
items.desc: BTC $73,055 (-0.2%); ETH $1,979 (-0.1%); SOL $80.65 (+0.1%). Market stabilizes after macro-panic.
items.summary: Bitcoin has stabilized around the $73K mark following yesterday's panic sell-off triggered by 'Trade War 2.0' fears. ETF outflows have slowed to $150M (from $400M peak), signaling that institutional 'smart money' is viewing the dip as a potential accumulation opportunity. Ethereum and Solana have also found local support, with SOL showing marginal gains as the network continues to capture DeFi market share from Ethereum L1.
items.importance: The $73K level is a 'neutral zone'—not oversold enough to trigger panic buying, not strong enough to signal a new ATH. The next macro catalyst will determine if this is a 'dead cat bounce' or the start of a new accumulation phase.
items.noteworthy: Watch the $73K level for 'double-bottom' support patterns. Monitor tomorrow's US PMI data as the next macro trigger. SOL/BTC pair is recovering—watch for SOL to regain momentum if the broader market stabilizes.

items.title: Anthropic & OpenAI: Product-Market Fit Achieved in 'Agentic' Era
items.desc: Analysts confirm both Anthropic and OpenAI have crossed the product-market fit threshold for autonomous AI agents.
items.summary: Simon Willison's analysis confirms that both Anthropic (Claude) and OpenAI (ChatGPT) have achieved genuine product-market fit for their respective 'Agentic' product lines. Anthropic has focused on 'Computer Use' and tool-calling, while OpenAI has focused on deep research and autonomous task completion. The competition is driving a rapid evolution in model capability and cost efficiency, with both companies now competing on 'Tokens per Dollar' rather than just benchmark scores.
items.importance: This signals a market-wide shift from 'AI as a chatbot' to 'AI as an autonomous agent.' For your own projects (netops, cyberdash), this means the tooling ecosystem for agentic workflows is now production-ready.
items.noteworthy: Evaluate deploying Claude Code or OpenAI's operator APIs for your most repetitive coding tasks. The 'Cost per Task' metric is now the key KPI for automation. The agentic era is here—start integrating it into your own infrastructure.
